decision_reason: medium-risk infra runner fix with a live repro and clear fix shape; codex 2/2 verified this session

packet.objective: GH issue #28: pi runner worktree isolation flags + regression test

command: TASK: In THIS worktree only, fix GitHub issue #28: PiAgentRunner writes can land in the project root repo instead of the assigned task worktree. LIVE EVIDENCE: a real plan attempt ran pi with cwd = an isolated git worktree, but pi (the pi-mono CLI) resolved its own project root/session and wrote files to the main project repository instead; the orchestrator saw zero changed paths in the worktree and blocked with test-author-produced-no-executable-checks. CONTEXT: backend/src/infra/runtime/cli_runner.py, class PiAgentRunner._build_cmd currently returns [pi, --model, m, -p, prompt, *extra_flags] and relies on subprocess cwd alone. The known-good hardened pi invocation elsewhere uses --no-session and --no-context-files to stop pi from resuming cached project state. FIX: (1) run pi --help (the binary is installed at /usr/bin/pi) and identify the flags that pin pi to the invocation directory and disable session/context reuse (--no-session, --no-context-files, and any explicit root/cwd flag if one exists); add the appropriate flags to _build_cmd as defaults while preserving extra_flags. (2) Add a regression test asserting the built pi command contains the isolation flags (see existing runner tests - grep tests/ for _build_cmd or PiAgentRunner usage; scripted fake CLI patterns live in tests/integration for the runner taxonomy). Do NOT modify backend/src/domain/. Do NOT try to add a post-run write-guard in this task - flags only, keep it minimal. VERIFY from backend/: uv run ruff check src tests && uv run mypy src && uv run pytest -q tests/unit tests/integration - all pass. Commit on branch task/pi-isolation/a1 with a conventional message referencing issue #28. Final message: the pi --help excerpt naming the chosen flags, files changed, test names, pytest summary line.

decision_reason: medium-risk cross-runtime safety guard; sequenced after #29 merged per same-concern rule; attempt 1 was a coordinator path error, nothing executed

packet.objective: post-run isolation guard: fail attempt loudly if the project main repo is dirtied by any runtime

command: TASK: In THIS worktree only, implement a runtime-agnostic post-run isolation guard (follow-up to issue #28, which added pi-specific flags - this is the enforcement those flags cannot give). PROBLEM: a CLI agent runtime (any of pi/claude/gemini) can write to absolute paths outside its assigned task worktree; the known live failure wrote into the PROJECT MAIN REPO (the repo the worktree was created from), and the orchestrator misreported it as the agent producing nothing. REQUIREMENT: after each agent attempt run and BEFORE the result is interpreted, verify the project main repository working tree gained no changes during the attempt; if it did, fail the attempt with TaskFailed and a message naming the stray paths (infrastructure failure, reuse the existing failure taxonomy - grep FailureKind usage; TOOL_ERROR pattern from the recent infrastructure-exit work in execution_handler is the precedent). DESIGN CONSTRAINTS: (1) NEVER modify backend/src/domain/. (2) Find the right seam yourself by reading how ExecutionHandler runs attempts and what the Workspace port exposes (GitBranchWorkspace in backend/src/infra/git/workspace.py knows the main repo path; the handler owns interpretation) - prefer capturing a light fingerprint before the run (git status porcelain of the MAIN repo, cheap) and comparing after, over anything heavier. (3) The guard must be a no-op for workspaces without a main-repo concept (LocalDirWorkspace/NoOpWorkspace fakes) so existing tests keep passing. (4) Do not scan node_modules-scale trees - git status is the tool. TESTS: extend the TDD/integration runner tests (backend/tests/integration/test_tdd_execution.py has scripted runner patterns) with a runner that deliberately writes into the main repo instead of the worktree and assert the attempt fails with the stray-path message and the plan blocks recoverably; also assert a clean run is unaffected. VERIFY from backend/: uv run ruff check src tests && uv run mypy src && uv run pytest -q tests/unit tests/integration - all pass. Commit on branch task/isolation-guard/a1 with a conventional message. Final message: seam chosen and why, files changed, test names, pytest summary line.

packet.objective: issue #30: render TaskContract constraints into build_task_prompt

command: TASK: In THIS worktree only, fix GitHub issue #30: the task prompt never tells the agent the constraints the orchestrator enforces. CONTEXT: backend/src/infra/runtime/cli_runner.py, build_task_prompt(task, spec) renders name, description, role, instructions, capabilities. The Task may carry task.contract (a TaskContract from src/domain/entities/execution_contracts.py) with objective, acceptance_criteria, allowed_scope, forbidden_scope, verification_commands, verification_strategy; the task also has tdd_stage semantics (test_authoring stage = write ONLY tests that fail for the right reason, never production files; implementation stage = make the frozen tests pass, never modify tests). LIVE EVIDENCE: a test-author agent scaffolded app/schemas/__init__.py because nothing told it tests-only; the scope guard then burned the whole attempt. FIX: extend build_task_prompt to render, WHEN task.contract is present, a Constraints section: contract objective, acceptance criteria (id+description), allowed paths, forbidden paths, verification commands (state the orchestrator runs these independently and self-reports are ignored), and stage expectations derived from the verification strategy and the role (look at how role_agent_ids/test_author vs implementer and task.tdd_stage flow through ExecutionHandler to know which stage this run is - if the stage is not cleanly available at prompt-build time, add the smallest parameter to build_task_prompt from the call site in CliAgentRunner.run, which receives the task snapshot). Contract-less tasks must produce the same prompt as today (exact backward compatibility). NEVER modify backend/src/domain/. TESTS: unit tests for build_task_prompt - contract-less unchanged; with contract: constraints rendered; test-author stage says tests-only; implementer stage says do-not-touch-tests. Find existing prompt tests via grep build_task_prompt tests/. VERIFY from backend/: uv run ruff check src tests && uv run mypy src && uv run pytest -q tests/unit tests/integration - all pass. Commit on branch task/prompt-contract/a1 referencing #30. Final message: files changed, test names, pytest summary line.

decision_reason: reasoner prompt+validation change with existing FakeLLMClient test harness; codex 5/5 verified

packet.objective: issue #31: feature-level TDD task granularity in enrichment prompt + submission validation

command: TASK: In THIS worktree only, fix GitHub issue #31: reasoner enrichment produces step-level TDD tasks that are incompatible with the runtime two-stage TDD model. CONTEXT: for tasks whose contract has verification_strategy=TDD, the ExecutionHandler ALREADY runs an internal test_author stage (writes failing tests, freezes bundle) then an implementer stage (makes them pass). But the enrichment step (reasoner.enrich_goal - see src/infra/reasoner/openai_reasoner.py, the submit_tasks tool handler, and src/infra/reasoner/runtime/prompts.py) lets the LLM emit alternating step tasks like Write failing tests for X / Implement X to make tests pass - which doubles the TDD cycle and makes the write-tests tasks implementer stage unsatisfiable (its verification command cannot pass until the NEXT task is done). LIVE EVIDENCE: plan blocked exactly this way. FIX in two layers: (1) PROMPT: extend the enrichment prompt/tool guidance so that for TDD strategy, tasks MUST be feature-level deliverable slices - the objective states the deliverable (e.g. Deliver validated Item schemas with passing tests), NEVER a write-tests-only or make-tests-pass-only step; state explicitly that the runtime itself performs the red/green split per task. (2) VALIDATION: in the submit_tasks handler where tool args are re-validated, reject (accepted:false with a clear error, allowing self-correction) submissions containing TDD tasks whose name or objective matches write-tests-only patterns (e.g. starts with or contains write failing test / write tests for / make .* tests pass as the whole objective) - keep the heuristic conservative to avoid false positives; put it next to the existing validation errors so the LLM self-corrects in the same session. NEVER modify backend/src/domain/. TESTS: extend the reasoner unit tests (tests/unit/reasoner/) and if applicable the FakeLLMClient flow (tests/fakes_llm.py + test_full_cycle_llm.py) - one test proving a step-level submission is rejected with the new error and a corrected feature-level submission is accepted; one proving the prompt text includes the granularity rule. VERIFY from backend/: uv run ruff check src tests && uv run mypy src && uv run pytest -q tests/unit tests/integration - all pass. Commit on branch task/enrich-granularity/a1 referencing #31. Final message: files changed, test names, pytest summary line.

packet.objective: issue #32: settle blocked active-cycle work when start_replan transitions to REPLANNING
prohibited: backend/src/domain/ (report if a missing transition forces escalation)

command: TASK: In THIS worktree only, fix GitHub issue #32: start_replan from a blocked cycle produces a stuck REPLANNING state. REPRO (live): plan has an active cycle with an execution_failure block on a failed task; POST /plans/{id}/replan succeeds -> phase=replanning, status=waiting; POST /plans/{id}/replanning/message then rejects: INVALID_EDIT replan discovery requires settled active-cycle work. REPLANNING is invisible to workers, so nothing ever settles the cycle; retry_stage/edit_task remain listed but the replan gate is a trap. FIX: when start_replan (grep request_replan / start_replan in backend/src/app/use_cases/ - likely control.py, request_replan.py, or cyclic_planning.py) transitions a plan whose active cycle holds a block and/or non-terminal tasks, settle that work as part of the SAME transaction: abandon the un-executed and failed tasks and resolve/clear the block using EXISTING aggregate transitions only (grep the aggregate for abandon_execution_task, resolve_block, finalize-abandon-style methods, cycle completion transitions - read how replanning_message settled-check decides, in conversation.py or cyclic_planning.py, and make the state it requires the state start_replan produces). If a genuinely required transition does not exist on the aggregate, DO NOT modify backend/src/domain/ - stop and report the missing transition in your final message instead. TESTS: unit/orchestration-level test on both truth backends if the suite pattern allows: blocked-cycle plan -> start_replan -> replanning_message proceeds (use the stub reasoner path; grep tests for request_replan usage to find the existing pattern); plus the negative that a clean RUNNING plan replans exactly as before. VERIFY from backend/: uv run ruff check src tests && uv run mypy src && uv run pytest -q tests/unit tests/integration - all pass. Commit on branch task/replan-settle/a1 referencing #32. Final message: files changed, the settled-check semantics you aligned with, test names, pytest summary line.
